page: Amazon Web Services m8in.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance belonging to the m8in server family. It is powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance features 8 virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores, with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture. It does not include local instance storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes, for which the instance is optimized. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 12 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this server.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8in.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads that require a steady ratio of compute to memory without the overhead of dedicated local storage or GPU hardware. This resource density makes it appropriate for workloads such as web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and moderate database hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors operating at 3.9 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance is optimized for network and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. This configuration provides a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for standard enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for applications such as web serving, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications.
